data.title: Nvidia CEO Urges Europe to Embrace Physical AI and Robotics
data.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has encouraged Europe to fully commit to the development of Physical AI and robotics, highlighting the continent's robust industrial foundation. The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. Huang emphasized the potential for Europe to lead in these technological advancements due to its strong industrial base. He believes that by leveraging its existing strengths, Europe can become a global leader in the AI and robotics sectors. Huang's comments come at a time when technological innovation is increasingly seen as a key driver of economic growth and competitiveness on the global stage.

data.title: STOCKS | Millennium Management Recruits Talent from Citadel Amid Asset Growth
data.description: Millennium Management has recruited four stock pickers from Citadel, highlighting the ongoing competition among multistrategy hedge funds for skilled personnel. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that this move comes as Millennium seeks to bolster its team to manage increasing assets. The recruitment underscores the fierce rivalry between hedge fund giants as they strive to enhance their investment capabilities and maintain growth momentum.

data.title: Middle East Tensions Influence Oil Prices, Trump Predicts Short-Term Impact
data.description:
Oil prices have been driven up by ongoing tensions in the Middle East. According to RTHK, U.S. President Donald Trump stated that the increase in oil prices is only slight and is expected to be a short-term phenomenon, predicting a return to lower prices soon. He also mentioned having a plan to ensure the safety of the Strait of Hormuz.

In an interview with U.S. media, Trump revealed that since the conflict began last Saturday, the United States has sunk 25 Iranian vessels, significantly impacting Iran's navy. When asked about the possibility of accepting a new religious leader to take over Iran's leadership, Trump said it depends on the individual, expressing no objection to a religious leader as long as they are fair, competent, and treat the U.S., Israel, and other Middle Eastern countries well.

An Iranian military spokesperson reiterated that the Strait of Hormuz remains under Iranian control and will not be closed. However, vessels associated with the United States or Israel are prohibited from passing through.

data.title: Tensions Rise as Israel Faces Pressure Over Oil Trade
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. Amid escalating tensions, Israel is under scrutiny for its stance on oil trade, with concerns about potential repercussions if actions are taken against their carriers. The situation has raised questions about the strategic benefits of maintaining a closed position, as Israel also relies on oil sales for economic stability. Observers suggest that diplomatic pressure may be necessary to encourage Israel to reconsider its approach, emphasizing the potential losses involved in continuing the current policy.

data.title: SuperRare Launches Generative Art Tokens with Dynamic Visuals
data.description: SuperRare has introduced 'Liquid Editions,' a new type of generative art ERC-20 tokens that visually change according to live on-chain market data. According to NS3.AI, these tokens incorporate liquidity pools and can be paired with companion ERC-721 'lens' NFTs, which alter the shared artwork view as trading activity evolves.

data.title: Cerebras Selects Morgan Stanley for IPO Leadership
data.description: Cerebras Systems has chosen Morgan Stanley to spearhead its initial public offering (IPO). Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this strategic move as the company prepares to enter the public market. Cerebras, known for its advanced artificial intelligence computing solutions, aims to leverage Morgan Stanley's expertise to navigate the complexities of the IPO process. This decision marks a significant step for Cerebras as it seeks to expand its market presence and capitalize on the growing demand for AI technologies. The company has not yet disclosed the timeline or specific details of the IPO, but the selection of Morgan Stanley indicates a commitment to a robust and well-managed public offering.

data.title: James Patterson Adapts Strategy Amid Declining Sales
data.description: Renowned author James Patterson is adjusting his approach in response to decreasing sales after decades of success in the thriller genre. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Patterson's shift towards collaborative projects and exploring the romantasy genre as part of his strategy to rejuvenate his market presence. Patterson's decision to diversify his writing style comes as he seeks to maintain his standing in the competitive literary market. His collaborations with other writers aim to bring fresh perspectives and expand his audience base. The move into romantasy represents a significant departure from his traditional thriller narratives, indicating Patterson's willingness to innovate and adapt to changing consumer preferences. As the literary landscape evolves, Patterson's strategic adjustments reflect a broader trend among established authors to explore new genres and collaborative opportunities to sustain their relevance and appeal.

data.title: Trump Signs Executive Order to Combat Cybercrime
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has signed an executive order focused on combating cybercrime, with a particular emphasis on addressing fraud and extortion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the administration's commitment to strengthening cybersecurity measures. The executive order outlines strategies to enhance the nation's defenses against cyber threats, aiming to protect both individuals and businesses from digital crimes. This move is part of a broader effort to bolster national security and safeguard economic interests in the digital age.

data.title: Tensions Rise Over Tanker Exchange Proposal
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X about a proposal suggesting a direct exchange of tankers, followed by a strategic move targeting Kharg Island. The suggestion reflects ongoing geopolitical tensions and highlights the complexities of international negotiations. The proposal underscores the urgency of resolving conflicts efficiently without prolonged engagements.

data.title: Senator Warren Criticizes SEC Settlement with Tron Founder, Calls for Stronger Safeguards
data.description:
US Senator Elizabeth Warren has expressed criticism over the US Securities and Exchange Commission's (SEC) settlement with Tron founder Justin Sun. According to NS3.AI, Warren highlighted that Sun allegedly invested $90 million in crypto assets linked to US President Donald Trump and his family. She emphasized the need for enhanced anti-corruption measures in the crypto market structure legislation currently under review by the Senate Banking Committee.

The article mentions that the SEC reached a separate settlement with Sun and his companies for $10 million. The pending legislation, which addresses issues such as tokenized equities, ethics, and stablecoin rewards, has seen its markup by the Senate Banking Committee postponed indefinitely.

data.title: Market Reactions Amid Middle East Tensions and Oil Price Fluctuations
data.description:
The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. Current market conditions suggest a disconnect between perceived risks and actual developments. The VIX index recently approached 30, while oil prices surged to around $90 per barrel, indicating significant market apprehension. However, recent geopolitical developments may alleviate some concerns.

Iran's missile and drone capabilities have reportedly diminished by approximately 90%, and its naval forces have suffered substantial losses. Additionally, China has urged Iran to de-escalate tensions, while the U.S. Navy is actively working to ensure safe passage in the region. These factors suggest that a worst-case scenario, such as the complete closure of the Strait of Hormuz, is becoming less likely.

In the event of short-term disruptions, floating storage options are expected to mitigate immediate supply concerns. As a result, the market may be overestimating the potential for prolonged instability in the region.

data.title: Iran's Ammunition Supplies Dwindle Amid Ongoing Conflicts
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X that Iran's ammunition supplies appear to be depleting as ongoing conflicts continue. This development has led to significant fluctuations in prices this week. As the situation evolves, probabilities and prices are shifting, prompting adjustments in positions.

data.title: Oracle and OpenAI Cancel Texas AI Data Center Expansion Plans
data.description: Oracle and OpenAI have decided to abandon their plans to expand a major AI data center in Texas. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that the decision was influenced by prolonged negotiations over financing and evolving requirements from OpenAI. The companies had initially aimed to enhance their capabilities in the region, but shifting priorities led to the cancellation of the project. The move reflects the dynamic nature of the AI industry, where rapid changes in technology and business strategies can impact long-term plans.

data.title: Iran Denies Closing Strait of Hormuz, Restricts Passage for Israeli and U.S. Vessels
data.description: Iran has not closed the Strait of Hormuz, according to a senior Iranian military spokesperson. However, vessels associated with Israel or the United States are not permitted to pass through the strategic waterway. According to Jin10, this statement was broadcasted by Iranian state television. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical maritime chokepoint, and any restrictions on passage could have significant implications for global oil shipments and regional security dynamics.

data.title: U.S. Consumer Credit Growth Slows in January
data.description: U.S. consumer credit increased by $8.05 billion in January, falling short of the expected $12 billion. According to Jin10, the previous month's figure was revised from $24.045 billion to $25.2 billion. This slowdown in credit growth may indicate a cautious approach by consumers amid economic uncertainties. The revised data suggests a more significant expansion in December than initially reported, highlighting fluctuations in consumer borrowing patterns.

data.title: Bitcoin's Resilience Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Economic Uncertainty
data.description:
As geopolitical tensions rise and global markets encounter new uncertainties, Bitcoin has demonstrated unexpected stability. According to Cointelegraph, while the Middle East faces deepening conflict and energy markets react to potential supply disruptions, Bitcoin has maintained its position relatively well compared to many traditional assets. This resilience prompts some observers to question whether Bitcoin is signaling something about the macroeconomic environment that markets have yet to fully recognize.

Arthur Hayes, co-founder of Maelstrom, recently shared his insights on the forces influencing the global economy and why the coming months could be crucial for financial markets. On the geopolitical front, Hayes suggests that investors might be underestimating the risks if the current conflict between the U.S. and Iran expands or prolongs. He notes that disruptions in energy flows could lead to ripple effects throughout the global economy, manifesting as higher oil prices, inflationary pressures, and increased market volatility.

Simultaneously, Hayes points to another significant disruption unfolding beneath the surface: artificial intelligence. He argues that AI could rapidly transform the labor market by replacing a substantial portion of knowledge workers, including lawyers, bankers, accountants, and analysts. If this transition occurs swiftly, it could result in widespread credit stress as households struggle to manage existing debt. Hayes ultimately believes that the global financial system typically responds to crises with liquidity measures, describing Bitcoin as a "liquidity smoke alarm."

data.title: Kraken's xStocks Platform Introduces xChange for Tokenized Equities
data.description: Kraken's xStocks platform has unveiled xChange, an onchain trading engine designed for over 70 tokenized equities on Ethereum and Solana. According to NS3.AI, these tokenized shares are backed 1:1 by underlying stocks held in custody, ensuring that their prices closely follow public market prices. Since its launch in June, xStocks has facilitated approximately $25 billion in total trading volume.
